'''Brad Schneider''' (born 15 February 2001) is an [[Australians|Australian]] professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|hb}} for the [[Dolphins (NRL)|Dolphins]] in the [[National Rugby League]] (NRL). He previously played for the [[Penrith Panthers]] (including their successful [[2024 NRL Grand Final]]) and the [[Canberra Raiders]].

==Background==
Schneider is of German heritage.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://twitter.com/RaidersCanberra/status/1506850096507666433/|title=Raiders Backgrounds|website=Twitter|date=24 March 2022}}</ref>. He was born in [[Adelaide]], South Australia and was raised in [[Townsville]], [[Queensland]], Australia.{{cn|date=October 2024}}

Schneider played junior rugby league for the Western Lions.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.townsvillebulletin.com.au/sport/western-lions-young-cubs-stand-tall-as-the-side-overcame-injury-toll/news-story/b77df0274e0ee1d508f5e7e4163c264b|title=Western Lions young cubs stand tall as the side overcame injury toll|date=30 June 2019|website=Townsville Bulletin|access-date=22 May 2021}}</ref> He attended [[Kirwan State High School]] before being signed by the [[Canberra Raiders]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.couriermail.com.au/news/townsville/former-kirwan-bears-skipper-brad-schneider-will-make-his-nrl-debut-on-saturday-night/news-story/3ff24c080011843e93cf382ae5ee0e87|title=Former Kirwan Bears skipper Brad Schneider will make his NRL debut on Saturday night|date=20 May 2021|website=The Courier Mail|access-date=22 May 2021}}</ref>

==Playing career==
In 2018, Schneider played for the [[Townsville Blackhawks]] in the [[Mal Meninga Cup]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://leagueunlimited.com/news/leagueunlimited-mm-cup-teams-2018-round-5/|title=QRL U18 Mal Meninga Cup - 2018 Round 5|date=3 April 2018|website=League Unlimited|access-date=22 May 2021}}</ref> In 2019, he captained Kirwan State High School in their [[NRL Schoolboy Cup]] final win over [[Westfields Sports High School]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2019/09/18/cowboys-prospect-trevilyan-stars-as-kirwan-take-schoolboy-cup-crown/|title=Cowboys prospect Trevilyan stars as Kirwan take Schoolboy Cup crown|date=18 September 2019|website=NRL|access-date=22 May 2021}}</ref>

===Canberra Raiders (2021-23)===
[[File:BRAD SCHNEIDER.jpg|thumb|Brad Schneider in 2021]]
In October 2019, Schneider signed with the [[Canberra Raiders]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.townsvillebulletin.com.au/sport/cowboys-lose-out-at-kirwan-high-leaders-sign-contracts-with-nrl-rivals/news-story/cf579cde6b20dd0d0f534f7c638893a2|title=Cowboys lose out at Kirwan High leaders sign contracts with NRL rivals|date=16 October 2019|website=Townsville Bulletin|access-date=22 May 2021}}</ref> In 2020, Schneider was set to play for the Raiders' [[Jersey Flegg Cup]] team before the season was cancelled due to the [[COVID-19 pandemic]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.raiders.com.au/news/2019/11/22/raiders-jersey-flegg-training-squad-named/|title=Raiders Jersey Flegg Training Squad Named|date=22 November 2019|website=Canberra Raiders|access-date=22 May 2021}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.raiders.com.au/news/2020/03/17/jersey-flegg-team-list-raiders-v-bulldogs/|title=Jersey Flegg Team List: Raiders v Bulldogs|date=17 March 2020|website=Canberra Raiders|access-date=22 May 2021}}</ref>

Schneider began the 2021 season playing for Canberra's [[NSW Cup]] team.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.raiders.com.au/news/2021/03/09/nsw-cup-team-list-raiders-v-magpies/|title=NSW Cup Team List: Raiders v Magpies|date=9 March 2021|website=Canberra Raiders|access-date=22 May 2021}}</ref> In Round 11 of the [[2021 NRL season]], Schneider made his first grade début for Canberra against the [[Melbourne Storm]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.raiders.com.au/news/2021/05/18/nrl-team-list-raiders-v-storm/|title=NRL Team List: Raiders v Storm|date=18 May 2021|website=Canberra Raiders|access-date=22 May 2021}}</ref>

Schneider made ten appearances for Canberra in the 2022 season, posting fifty points in total which consisted of scoring one try and twenty-three successful goals. He recorded a 79.31% goal kicking percentage in 2022.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.raiders.com.au/teams/nrl-premiership/canberra-raiders/brad-schneider/|title=Canberra Raiders Stats - Brad Schneider|website=www.raiders.com.au}}</ref> Schneider won the Peter Mulholland NRL Rookie of the Year Award in 2022.<ref>{{cite web|url= https://www.raiders.com.au/about/awards/|title=Canberra Raiders Award Winners  - Brad Schneider|website=www.raiders.com.au}}</ref>

===Hull Kingston Rovers (2023)===
It was revealed on 6 July, that Schneider had been released by Canberra effective immediately, to be subsequently signed by [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] until the end of the 2023 season.<ref>{{cite web|url= https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/brad-schneider-hull-kr-nrl-8572832|title=The Robins swoop for Schneider - Award-winning arrival|website=www.hulldailymail.co.uk}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url= https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/brad-schneider-hull-kr-2023-8573024|title=Brad Schneider arrives at Rovers - Mikey Lewis and Rowan Milnes put on Hull KR warning|website=www.hulldailymail.co.uk}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url= https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-kr-brad-schneider-canberra-30405170|title=Hull KR confirm signing of Canberra Raiders' Brad Schneider - Schneider to ease injury blow|website=www.mirror.co.uk}}</ref> A deal in which contained a recall clause, meaning that he could be recalled by Canberra if one of Canberra's halfbacks suffered an injury that would keep them from the field for four weeks or longer.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/brad-schneider-hull-kr-recall-8579971|title=Interesting Recall Arraignment - Brad Schneider deal|website=www.hulldailymail.co.uk}}</ref> On 14 July, Schneider made his first appearance for Hull KR in a [[Super League]] fixture against the [[Leeds Rhinos]] at [[Headingley Rugby Stadium|Headingley]]. Schneider started the game at scrum-half, scored a try on début, made an assist and slotted over a field goal in [[Golden point]] extra time to win the game 18-19.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/watch/video/sports/rugby-league/12921224/brad-schneider-scores-try-and-kicks-winning-drop-goal-in-dream-debut/|title=Brad Schneider scores try and kicks winning drop-goal in dream début - Brad Schneider|website=www.skysports.com}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/66192912|title=Leeds Rhinos 18-19 Hull KR - Brad Schneider kicks golden point winner for visitors|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>

On 23 July, in his next game at the same venue, he repeated the feat against the [[Wigan Warriors]] in the semi-final of the [[Challenge Cup]], once again slotting over a match-winning field goal from forty metres in golden point extra time to win the game 11-10. Schneider also kicked his first goal for Rovers in the same game.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/12926284/challenge-cup-hull-kr-beat-wigan-in-semi-final-with-brad-schneider-kicking-golden-point-drop-goal/|title=Hull KR beat Wigan in semi-final with Brad Schneider kicking golden point field goal|website=www.skysports.com}}</ref> On 9 August, Schneider was named Glen's Vodka Super League Player of the Month for July.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.superleague.co.uk/article/3645/schneider-voted-glens-player-of-the-month|title=Player of the Month - Brad Schneider|website=www.superleague.co.uk}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://twitter.com/SuperLeague/status/1689290725987332096?ref_src=twsrc%5Egoogle%7Ctwcamp%5Eserp%7Ctwgr%5Etweet|title=Betfred Super League Player of the Month for July - Brad Schneider|website=www.twitter.com}}</ref> On 12 August, Schneider played for Hull Kingston Rovers in their 16-17 golden point extra time loss to the [[Leigh Leopards]] in the [[2023 Challenge Cup final]] at [[Wembley Stadium]]. Schneider scored eight of Rovers' sixteen points, by kicking two conversions and two penalty goals.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/66463438|title=Challenge Cup final Hull KR 16-17 Leigh Leopards - Leigh claim first Wembley final win in 52 years|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/match/EVP4267769|title=Challenge Cup final - Score|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>

On 12 September, it was announced that Schneider would return to the National Rugby League for the 2024 season and beyond, to play for [[Penrith Panthers|Penrith]] on a two-year contract.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/66784480|title=Brad Schneider - Hull KR half-back to join NRL side Penrith Panthers|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2023-transfer-centre-penrith-panthers-sign-paul-alamoti-brad-schneider-bulldogs-raiders-contracts-player-movement/news-story/32a8e3b5bd55d4cc9647a4604b450693/|title=Panthers poach Bulldogs young gun, Raiders playmaker in double boost: NRL Transfer Centre|website=www.foxsports.com.au}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url= https://www.penrithpanthers.com.au/news/2023/09/12/panthers-sign-alamoti-and-schneider/|title=Alamoti and Schneider sign with Panthers - NRL|website=www.penrithpanthers.com.au}}</ref> On 22 September, in the last game of the regular season, Schneider scored a brace of tries and successfully kicked eight goals in the Robins' 12-56 triumph over [[Wakefield Trinity]]. Securing said victory, would mean that Schneider helped Hull KR finish in fourth position overall after twenty-seven rounds.<ref>{{cite web|url= https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/66870868|title=Super League: Wakefield Trinity 12-56 Hull KR - Robins finish fourth to secure home play-off|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref> On 29 September, Schneider played in Hull Kingston Rovers' Super League play-off eliminator against the Leigh Leopards, securing a 20-6 success.<ref>{{cite web|url= https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/66913472|title=Super League - Hull KR book semi-final place|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref> On 7 October, Schneider played his final game for Hull KR, losing in the Super League play-off semi-final to the Wigan Warriors at the [[DW Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url= https://twitter.com/hullkrofficial/status/1710657149460771023?ref_src=twsrc%5Egoogle%7Ctwcamp%5Eserp%7Ctwgr%5Etweet/|title=Hull KR season ends - Rovers come up short|website=www.twitter.com}}</ref>

===Penrith Panthers (2024-2025)===
Schneider played fourteen matches for Penrith in the [[2024 NRL season]]. On 6 October, he won the [[2024 NRL Grand Final]] with them.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2024-10-06/nrl-grand-final-2024-quick-hits-biting-bunker-penrith-panthers/104422068/|title=NRL grand final 2024 quick hits: Biting allegation, Panthers halves swansong and a controversial bunker decision|website=www.abc.net.au}}</ref> Schneider played another eleven games with Penrith in 2025.
[[File:Brad Schneider DOLPHINS.jpg|thumb|Brad Schneider in 2026]]

===Dolphins (2026)===
On 14 August 2025, the Dolphins announced that they had signed Schneider for the 2026 season.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2025-08-13 |title=Schneider to join Dolphins for 2026 |url=https://www.dolphinsnrl.com.au/news/2025/08/14/schneider-to-join-dolphins-for-2026/ |access-date=2025-08-15 |website=The Dolphins |language=en}}</ref>

==Achievements and accolades==
===Individual===

*2022 Peter Mulholland NRL Rookie of the Year: '''Winner'''<ref>{{cite web|url= https://www.raiders.com.au/news/2022/09/12/2022-peter-mulholland-rookie-of-the-year/#:~:text=Congratulations%20to%20Brad%20Schneider%2C%20our,of%20the%20Year%20award%20winner!|title=2022 Rookie of the Year - Brad Schneider|website=www.raiders.com.au}}</ref>
*2025 Australia Prime Ministers XIII <ref>{{cite web|url= https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2025-australian-mens-and-womens-prime-ministers-xiii-revealed-keaon-koloamatangi-rabbitohs-kyle-flanagan-news-videos-png/news-story/33dbe9bb0efd10ed32727d6bc0600365/#:~:text=2025%20Prime%20Ministers%20XIII%20Squad|title=2025 PMXIII squad|website= www.foxsports.com.au}}</ref>
